title: Augmented Human Development Index
subtitle: The Augmented Human Development Index (AHDI) is a summary measure of achievement in four key dimensions of human development: a long and healthy life, being knowledgeable, being free and having a decent standard of living.
caption:
Augmented Human Development Index, 2020
The Augmented Human Development Index (AHDI) is a summary measure of achievement in four key dimensions of human development: a long and healthy life, being knowledgeable, being free and having a decent standard of living.

The world map displays the Augmented Human Development Index for 2020, using a continuous color scale to represent scores ranging from 0 to 0.8, where darker shades of blue indicate higher levels of development. The visualization reveals a distinct geographic distribution of human development achievements across the globe. The highest values, falling within the 0.7 to 0.8 band, are concentrated in North America, Western Europe, and Oceania, specifically visible in the United States, Canada, Australia, New Zealand, and nations such as Norway, Sweden, and Germany. Japan also appears in this top tier. Moving down the scale, countries in the 0.5 to 0.6 range include major economies in Latin America like Brazil and Argentina, as well as Russia and China. Much of Eastern Europe and parts of the Middle East fall into the intermediate bands between 0.4 and 0.6. The lowest scores, represented by the lightest shades in the 0.2 to 0.4 range, are predominantly located across sub-Saharan Africa, including nations like Nigeria, Ethiopia, and the Democratic Republic of the Congo, as well as parts of South Asia such as India and Pakistan. Greenland and a few other territories are marked with hatching to indicate no data is available. The spatial pattern shows a clear gradient where the darkest blue regions cluster in the Global North, while the lightest shades dominate the Global South.
